channel.title: Context Engineering Daily - Prompt Engineering
channel.description: Latest Prompt Engineering news in Context Engineering

item.title: PromptMN: Pseudo Prompting Language
item.description: arXiv:2606.17164v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Prompting has become the primary interface between humans and generative AI, yet many natural language prompts remain fragile: roles, goals, constraints, and expected outputs are often buried in prose or left implicit. In agentic and software developm...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, fine-tuning, context, prompt, instruction | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: RepSelect: Robust LLM Unlearning via Representation Selectivity
item.description: arXiv:2606.17168v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Making large language models (LLMs) deeply forget specific knowledge and values without sacrificing general capabilities remains a central challenge in unlearning. However, current methods are easily reversed by fine-tuning or few-shot prompting, sugg...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM, fine-tuning, prompt, prompting, few-shot | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: From Parasocial Scripts to Dyadic Persistence in Autonomous AI-Agent Communities
item.description: arXiv:2606.17174v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: While parasocial interactions (PSIs) and parasocial relationships (PSRs) have been studied in conventional media settings, we investigate whether PSI- (colloquial) relational cues also exist in online communities where both sides are autonomous AI age...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM, context, few-shot, model, large language model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Are you speaking my languages? On spoken language adherence in multimodal LLMs
item.description: arXiv:2606.17281v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: While Large Language Model (LLM) based Automatic Speech Recognition (ASR) enables seamless multilingual use, models often misidentify the output language, compromising transcription fidelity and downstream application quality. To preserve flexibility ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM, CoT, fine-tuning, multimodal, chain-of-thought | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Context-Engineering - "Context engineering is the delicate art and science of filling the context window with just the right information for the next step." — Andrej Karpathy. A frontier, first-principles handbook inspired by Karpathy and 3Blue1Brown for moving beyond prompt engineering to the wider discipline of context design, orchestration, and optimization.
item.description: "Context engineering is the delicate art and science of filling the context window with just the right information for the next step." — Andrej Karpathy. A frontier, first-principles handbook inspired by Karpathy and 3Blue1Brown for moving beyond prompt engineering to the wider discipline of context design, orchestration, and optimization.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, context window, prompt, prompt engineering | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: MemSlides: A Hierarchical Memory Driven Agent Framework for Personalized Slide Generation with Multi-turn Local Revision
item.description: arXiv:2606.17162v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Personalized presentation generation requires more than conditioning on a current prompt or template: agents must preserve stable user preferences across tasks, retain newly introduced preferences and constraints during multi-turn revision, and carry ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> memory, template, alignment, vision, prompt | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Claude-Code-Everything-You-Need-to-Know - The ultimate all-in-one guide to mastering Claude Code. From setup, prompt engineering, commands, hooks, workflows, automation, and integrations, to MCP servers, tools, and the BMAD method—packed with step-by-step tutorials, real-world examples, and expert strategies to make this the global go-to repo for Claude mastery.
item.description: The ultimate all-in-one guide to mastering Claude Code. From setup, prompt engineering, commands, hooks, workflows, automation, and integrations, to MCP servers, tools, and the BMAD method—packed with step-by-step tutorials, real-world examples, and expert strategies to make this the global go-to repo for Claude mastery.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> step-by-step, prompt, example, prompt engineering, tool | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: AlphaCodium - Official implementation for the paper: "Code Generation with AlphaCodium: From Prompt Engineering to Flow Engineering""
item.description: Official implementation for the paper: "Code Generation with AlphaCodium: From Prompt Engineering to Flow Engineering""<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> paper, prompt, prompt engineering | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: optillm - Optimizing inference proxy for LLMs
item.description: Optimizing inference proxy for LLMs<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
